A01: Unauthenticated GET /api/donations returns 401/422
A01: Donor role cannot accept donations (403 RBAC check)
A01: NGO role cannot create donations (403 RBAC check)
A01: Volunteer cannot assign self to non-existent donation (403/400)
A01: NGO cannot assign volunteer (403 RBAC)
A02: JWT secret is not exposed in API response
A02: Passwords are not returned in API responses
A03: SQL injection in login email field returns 401 (not 500)
A03: SQL injection in password field does not bypass auth
A03: XSS payload in signup username is sanitized or rejected
A03: HTML injection payload in donation address does not cause 500
A04: API does not expose internal stack trace on bad request
A04: API does not accept donations with negative quantities gracefully
A05: API returns proper HTTP status codes (not always 200)
A05: API /api/auth/profile without token returns 401/422
A05: CORS is enabled (server responds with CORS headers)
A06: Server does not expose Flask/Python version in headers
A07: Tampered JWT (modified payload) is rejected by API
A07: Empty Bearer token string is rejected by API
A07: Malformed Authorization header is rejected
A07: Brute-force – wrong password multiple times returns 401 consistently
A07: JWT token format has three parts (header.payload.signature)
A08: API accepts JSON content-type only (rejects form-data for signup)
A09: Failed login attempt returns structured JSON error
A09: Unauthorized API access returns JSON error (not HTML)
A10: SSRF – malicious URL in address field does not trigger server request
Mass Assignment – extra fields in signup are ignored
Parameter pollution – duplicate email params in login
Oversized payload in login body does not crash server
Unicode/null bytes in login do not crash server
Signup with invalid role value is rejected or stored as-is (no privilege escalation)
Full lifecycle with valid tokens – all role boundaries enforced
